item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is exceedingly disgusting, and excellently composed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In answer to your questions:

1) My logic is that because the Foundation has made the Meat Circus association, they have assumed that it's a roman numeral (which is another Meat Circus calling card).

2) In-universe, the Foundation (because they're not genre-savvy) is yet to determine a pattern behind the D-Class reactions - they'll keep testing until they work it out. Out-of-universe, of course, I need testing to continue so that I can conjure the image of naked D-Class as a Containment Procedures hook :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be damned if I wasn't uncomfortable while reading the Description. I'd put it on par with SCP-439 in terms of making me very, very uncomfortable while I was reading it. That was some great imagery. In addition, my reaction to the testing log could be described as "confused but very afraid."

I didn't really get anything from the interview log though. It has good imagery, but I found myself more confused than frightened by what was going on in there. It might just be me, though.

Overall, very, very frightening. A classic psul. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article evoked a visceral anxiety in me and the interview made my arms tremble. It’s like if Stanley Kubrick had produced The Human Centipede.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, I, uh, really don't like Meat Circus stuff in general, but once I got past the yuck, I've got two little questions. 1. Why is it assumed to be a Roman Numeral L rather than a letter L? (Is it because the Meat Circus, and presumably this object are Russian, so a Roman Numeral is more plausible in this case than a Latin alphabet L?) 2. Why is there continuing monthly testing of this object? I didn't really get the interest in continuing to see a series of D-class becoming body-horror facepaint art critics, nor did I find their reactions or the object's reactions, to change or escalate in any way that suggested a need for ongoing testing.

item.title: Roll up, roll up - welcome to the Meat Circus!
item.content:encoded:
This article is a little introduction to the Meat Circus - a classic GoI from the SCP Russian branch. I've included a few links to get you familiar with their great work.

Further links will hopefully follow as my wonderful team puts up their work. Many thanks to them: PeppersGhost, Captain Kirby and Nederbird.

Don't forget to vote on the INT page as part of the contest: http://scp-int.wikidot.com/scp-4642

And you can read more Meat Circus contest work at SCP-4775
